Title: Integrated transcriptomic and metabolomic analyses uncover the key pathways of Limonium bicolor in response to salt stress

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated at 100 mM NaCl (S1); reduced under higher salt (S3/S4) relative to S1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in seedlings/leaves under low salt; lower under high salt
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mitigates oxidative damage under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS detoxification under low salt conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOD gene expression paralleled enzyme activity under salt treatments. 2. Expression/activity peaked at S1 (100 mM NaCl). 3. Lower activity under high salt coincided with ROS accumulation and damage.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of antioxidant enzyme genes; enzyme activity assays; physiological ROS measurements
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally profiled in Results, but not a direct nutrient-metabolism focus gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated at S1; decreased under higher salt
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in seedlings/leaves at low salt
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxifies peroxides and reduces oxidative damage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced POD expression/activity under low salt supports salt adaptation; reduced activity under high salt accompanies oxidative stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. POD activity and transcript level increased under S1. 2. Activity declined at higher salinity. 3. Pattern matched physiological ROS changes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; enzyme activity assays; ROS/MDA measurements
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a central nutrient-metabolism core gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest under S1, reduced under S3/S4
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated CAT expression/activity under low salt contributes to oxidative protection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAT transcript and activity increased at 100 mM NaCl. 2. High salt reduced CAT capacity. 3. Reduced CAT at high salt coincided with elevated H2O2.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest at S1; lower under stronger salt stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in leaves/seedlings at low salt
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Part of antioxidant network removing H2O2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced APX expression/activity under low salt helps maintain redox balance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APX transcript levels paralleled enzyme activity. 2. Maximal induction occurred at S1. 3. Activity declined under high salt where ROS accumulated.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream branch-entry activation step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phenolic acids and lignin-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step implied but not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern consistent with increased phenolic metabolite accumulation under S1 and S3 relative to S0
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Salt-responsive in seedlings/leaves; elevated under treatments associated with phenolic accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenolic compounds including caffeoyl shikimic acid, coniferin, coniferyl alcohol, sinapic acid-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense, stress adaptation, potential medicinal value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased phenylpropanoid metabolite accumulation under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL was identified as a key gene in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis under salt stress. 2. Its expression pattern matched increases in multiple phenolic metabolites under S1 and S3. 3. This supports a role in promoting salt-responsive phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated RNA-seq and metabolomics; pathway mapping; qRT-PCR support for pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM and 300 mM NaCl versus control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper links 4CL expression to phenolic accumulation but does not provide gene ID or direct transgenic validation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for sinapate- and coniferyl/sinapyl-related phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway gene implied, not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern consistent with accumulation of coniferin, coniferyl alcohol, sinapic acid, sinapaldehyde, syringin-related metabolites under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and stress defense; medicinally relevant phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of salt-responsive phenolic compounds in L. bicolor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT was highlighted as a key gene underlying phenolic metabolite changes. 2. Its transcript pattern agreed with increased phenylpropanoid compounds in S1 and S3. 3. It likely supports methylated monolignol/sinapate derivative accumulation under salt stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No accession or biochemical assay reported in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies intermediates connected to phenolic and downstream flavonoid/monolignol metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased in S1 and increased in S3, matching flavonoid trend described by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and osmotic stress alleviation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern correlates with reduced flavonoids under low salt and increased flavonoids under high salt
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT was one of the key genes whose expression followed flavonoid metabolite trends. 2. Expression decreased under S1 and increased under S3. 3. This supports involvement in salt-responsive secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role inferred from pathway placement and correlation; direct enzyme assay not performed.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core entry step to flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of multiple flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/committed-step enzyme for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased under S1 and increased under S3; matched changes in flavonoid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonoids including naringenin chalcone, naringenin, pinobanksin and related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and reduction of ROS damage under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation under high salt; reduced expression under low salt corresponds to lower flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS was identified as a key flavonoid biosynthetic gene responsive to salt. 2. CHS expression decreased in S1 and increased in S3 in parallel with flavonoid metabolite levels. 3. The study links CHS induction to accumulation of antioxidant flavonoids under high salt.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated RNA-seq and metabolomics; qRT-PCR validation of structural genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salt stress, especially 300 mM high salt
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also discussed with literature evidence that CHS overexpression improves salt tolerance in other plants.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased in S1 and increased in S3, mirroring flavonoid accumulation pattern
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidants contributing to ROS scavenging and stress acclimation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS expression tracked the flavonoid profile under salt treatments. 2. It was reduced under low salt and induced under high salt. 3. This supports a role in producing antioxidant flavonoids during severe salt stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct enzymology in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for flavonols and related flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under salt stress according to Discussion; associated with increased flavonoid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonoids including pinobanksin, naringenin-derived products, eriodictyol chalcone-related branch metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and mitigation of ROS damage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to salt-induced increase of flavonoid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Discussion identifies F3H as a key gene upregulated under salt stress. 2. Increased expression was associated with higher levels of several flavonoids. 3. The gene is implicated in antioxidant flavonoid accumulation during salt adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Discussion rather than emphasized in Results figure text; still treated as core due to explicit gene-metabolite linkage.
